code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # The first subject is treated as the main entity of the sentence.
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase that describes the object relative to the main subject.
    # The phrase does NOT repeat the subject; it can be used as a clause or a relative phrase.
    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from object strings
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Order in which attributes should appear (more important/defining attributes first)
    _priority = [
        "bedCount", "floorCount", "height", "architecturalStyle", "buildingType",
        "architect", "owner", "developer", "tenant", "currentTenants", "cost",
        "floorArea", "location", "region", "state", "country", "isPartOf",
        "address", "inaugurationDate", "buildingStartDate", "completionDate",
        "yearOfConstruction", "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber", "significantBuilding",
        "significantProject", "origin", "formerName", "leader", "chancellor",
        "governmentType", "capital", "language", "leaderTitle", "currency",
        "ethnicGroup", "website", "foundationPlace", "demonym", "numberOfRooms",
        "keyPerson", "architecture", "postalCode"
    ]

    # Mapping from predicate to a phrase that describes the object relative to the main subject.
    # The phrase does NOT repeat the subject; it can be used as a clause or a relative phrase.
    attr_templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "with an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction started in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "with {obj} floors",
        "location": "located in {obj}",
        "cost": "costing {obj}",
        "floorArea": "with a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "standing {obj} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "which is a {obj}",
        "developer": "developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "has tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
        "country": "in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "addressed at {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "with {obj} beds",
        "region": "in the region {obj}",
        "state": "in the state {obj}",
        "website": "website is {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "governed by {obj}",
        # More natural phrasing for leadership relations
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "chancellor": "is headed by {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        # More natural currency phrasing
        "currency": "currency is {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "has significant project {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "people are called {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "features {obj}",
        "postalCode": "postal code {obj}",
    }

    parts = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = attr_templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            # Some templates need the subject (e.g., leader) to be placed after the object.
            if "{subj}" in tmpl:
                phrase = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
            else:
                phrase = tmpl.format(obj=triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            phrase = f"{triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        parts.append(phrase)

    # Build the sentence: start with the main subject, then add descriptive parts.
    if not parts:
        return f"{main_subject}."

    # Separate parts that are full sentences (contain a verb) from simple attributes.
    # For simplicity, treat everything as an attribute clause and join with commas,
    # inserting “and” before the last clause.
    if len(parts) == 1:
        description = parts[0]
    else:
        description = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]

    sentence = f"{main_subject} {description}."
    # Capitalize the first character (in case main_subject is not capitalized)
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
